# Methods: data {#sec-methods-data}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — an outline of intended content and its source artifacts, not finished prose.
:::

*[Scaffold]* The dataset and how it enters the models.

## The trial and design {#sec-methods-data-trial}

*[Scaffold]* The randomised waitlist-crossover trial of the reading and language
intervention [@burgoyne2012rli]; the immediate vs wait-list arms and the crossover; the
four measurement waves; n ≈ 54. Cross-reference the design overview in @sec-study.

## Measures and scale {#sec-methods-data-measures}

*[Scaffold]* Each outcome is a **raw bounded count** (number of items correct), not an
age-normed score — so the maturation/exposure signal is fully present and the
Beta-Binomial likelihood (@sec-methods-likelihood) is the natural fit. List the
measures (vocabulary, grammar, speech, early reading), each with its item total. *Source:*
`data_variables.py`; measurement audit `notes/202606171000-measurement-sensitivity-audit.md`.

## Missing data, leakage, and exclusions {#sec-methods-data-missing}

*[Scaffold]* No outlier exclusion unless justified on the model page; the complete-case
caveat for the SES-adjusted comparators (@sec-robustness); GroupKFold by `subject_id` for
the exploratory step (@sec-gb) so repeated measures on a child cannot leak across folds.
